title: 'Drawing of a shield with Tassels on each side and six circular panels upon it...
description: 'Drawing of a shield with Tassels on each side and six circular panels upon it showing respectively on each Panel The Star of India, a Dragon, Crescent & Star. Japanese ornamental star, a Lion & sword with sun. A Corean star - below and at the lower portion of the shield, a dark background of stars with the words India, China, Turkey, Corea, Persia, Japan, upon a Riband. The sun is rising from the back of upper part of the shield with its rays spread over the sky, on the Rays are the words The Orient, the whole surrounded by a fancy border. (Copy attached)'. Copyright owner of work: Robert Bar

title: Sir Alexander Hosie, Travels Abroad
description: A series of twenty-five articles written in instalments by Sir Alexander Hosie, under the general heading Travels Abroad. The articles give an account of a world tour, undertaken 1919-20, taking in Canada, Japan, Korea, China, Hong Kong, Singapore, Malaysia and India, and returning to Sandown, the Isle of Wight. Chinese characters to front cover, and colour illustration verso of figure with sword. The articles are as follows: (1) Without title, beginning: "In accordance with my promise I am sending you some rough notes of our journey to the Far East which may be of interest to you, the readers
context: Archive of the Royal Society for Asian Affairs.Miscellaneous Material.

title: Hikayet-i arba'in subh u mesa - حكايت اربعين صبح و مسا
description: This volume contains an Ottoman Turkish translation of the well-known tale entitled in ArabicḤikāyat arbaᶜīn ṣubḥ wa masāᵓ,by Aḥmad Maṣrī. The story is also commonly known as The Story of the Forty Viziers (Hikayet-i Kırk Vezir or Kırk Vezir Hikayesi). It is an expansion of an old Indian tale, the Persian version of which is called Kitāb-i Sindbād or Sindbād-nāmah. The main narrative is the same in both: a young prince, falsely accused by his stepmother, is under a death sentence. He is defended in turn by the king's Vezirs (seven in the original work, forty in the expanded version), who, in t
context: British Library Ottoman Manuscript Collection.

title: Wreck of the S.S. 'Dayavati' of the B.S.N.Co. Ltd. Mulpy Rocks, The South Canara Coast. Photographer: Venn, T.W.
description: On her usual call at Mulpy, in a dense morning fog, she missed the entrance to the haven by a few hundred yards and went on the rocks. Though proceeding at slow speed at the time she struck, efforts to get the ship free were unavailing. The sea was calm and passengers were landed in the starboard lifeboats. Two of her port boats can be seen still in the davits.

title: 24 Oct 1815 [Received 7 Dec]. New York per the John Tobin, Liverpool. William to...
description: 24 Oct 1815 [Received 7 Dec]. New York per the John Tobin, Liverpool. William to father and mother - landed after tedious voyage - 65 days - no 2 fine days after 1st 3 weeks. Season remarkably tempestuous but vessel very good; to dine with Mr E M - sends messages. Staying at Inn, the Washington Hall - may stay some time because city is full of strangers that no single decent bed unoccupied in a boarding house. Three fellow passengers share with him room big enough to sleep 100. Accommodations temporary only put up to answer amazing demand with town so full of customers. Hopes to better himself
context: Longsdon of Little Longstone Family and Estate Papers.CORRESPONDENCE.

description: Folios 555-556: Philip Durham, HMS Anson, Gibraltar. Duplicate of letters left at Gibraltar to be forwarded to England. 27 June 1800: Reports that this morning at daylight he delivered a large convoy of between 40 and 50 ships to the Straits of Gibraltar. On the approach they got under the batteries where they were covered by 25 gun boats who together with the forts very much annoyed them. Nevertheless, with the assistance of 2 Gibraltar boats they captured 8, one of which was later retaken. They proved to be Spanish ships from Malaga bound for Cadiz. description: Folios 228-230: John Dilkes, HMS Madras, off Portland. title: Journal of Sir Charles Monck's Travels in Germany, Venice, Greece
description: This journey, which was a combination of Grand Tour and honeymoon, took place during the Napoleonic wars, yet, such was the nature of warfare at that period that Sir Charles was able to carry out his itinerary without hindrance and his diary scarcely refers to the war or to enemy operations. The fleas which infested Greek inns and lodgings caused more discomfort than the Emperor's soldiers and the Mediterranean storms were a greater danger than the French Men of War. Sir Charles writes interesting,and frequently amusingly on architecture by personalities, plants, prices and a variety of topics
context: Papers of the Middleton family of Belsay.

title: Folios 28-30: Surgeon's general remarks. The surgeon joined the ship at Deptford on 9 October...
description: Folios 28-30: Surgeon's general remarks. The surgeon joined the ship at Deptford on 9 October 1839, the guard, under Captain Hill of the 96th Regiment, joined the following day and the ship sailed on the 12th, arriving in Kingstown Harbour [Dun Laoghaire] on 23 October 1839. On 28th 100 convicts were embarked from Kilmainham Jail and on the 30th the surgeon inspected a further 60, several of whom were rejected, being infected with opthalmia, 2 with ill conditioned ulcers and 1 with pulmonic disease. On 6 November 1839, 45 more were sent on board and 3 were returned as unlikely to survive the v
context: Admiralty and predecessors: Office of the Director General of the Medical Department of the Navy and predecessors: Medical Journals. Convict Ships etc. Medical and surgical journal of the convict ship

title: Letter from W. A. Glynn, Montpelier House, Ilfracomb, Devon, to his uncle, Sir William Oglander, Baronet, Parnham, Beaminster, Dorset
description: "I have heard, papers have been posted by you, wherein you claim the Manor of Nettleston, as your property. I wish to write to you on this subject, in a manner so respectful to yourself no person could think it otherwise - in an affectionate manner too - if you will allow me. Avoiding the slightest tendency to a litigious spirit. Indeed, when my late Father, subsequently, when my poor Mother, and Admiral Glynn my Uncle, disputed this very manor with Dr. Anwyl of Priory, my Uncle, Mr. John Oglander of Merton Coll., represented to my Mother, by letter dated thence, the small value of the manoria
context: The Oglander Collection.PRIVATE CORRESPONDENCE AND PAPERS.

description: Folio 21: 11th-13th June 1821, received 172 male convicts from the Justitia, berthed them and supplied them with beds, bowls etc. Folio 22: 17th June 1821, at noon Mr. Capper came on board to examined the ship. Folio 22: 22nd June 1821, got underweigh at 9 am. Folio 22: 23rd June 1821,at 9 am. discharged the pilot in the Downs and sent a letter by him to the Navy Board. title: Folios 392-393: Thomas Browne, HMS Ulysses, St. Thomas's. Encloses letters from Captain Tailour, HMS Comus...
description: Folios 392-393: Thomas Browne, HMS Ulysses, St. Thomas's. Encloses letters from Captain Tailour, HMS Comus detailing his trip up the River Calabar and detaining 8 Portuguese and Spanish slave vessels. Masters of several merchant ships on passage from England had advised that slave traders were given preference on the Calabar when they were taking in their cargoes of palm oil and he had ordered Captain Tailour to take measurers to support these traders on his arrival. Suggests that the fear of a second visit would deter the slave ships. Encloses a list of the ships detained and the number of sl

title: Normal School of Science South Kensington SW My dear Bess I was delighted to get...
description: Normal School of Science South Kensington SW My dear Bess I was delighted to get your grand letter last week. I have been waiting this week to hear of the result of my application to Owen's college, Manchester; but haven't heard decidedly as yet. You know I intended to go in for the Fellowship of the College, rather an ambitious thing to attempt perhaps; but it would be a grand think for me if I got it as it is a great honour to be made a fellow of a college like Owens, the head college of the Victoria University. the money is not great. I shall if I get it have £100 a year for 2 years and hav
context: Sir Thomas Henry Holland (1868 - 1947); Miscellaneous Family Letters.Letters from Sir Thomas.

title: 1 item extracted from FO 371/12445. 'Manchurian Railways': map showing parts of China, Japan, Korea,...
description: 1 item extracted from FO 371/12445. 'Manchurian Railways': map showing parts of China, Japan, Korea, and the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics (areas now in the Russian Federation), with roads, towns, telegraphs and canals. Reference table to railways, indicating the gauge and ownership of each part of the system. Copy of a map produced by the War Office in 1926 or 1928.
context: Public Record Office: Maps and plans extracted to flat storage from various series of records of the Foreign Office.

title: 1 item extracted from FO 371/6620 (folio 128). East Asia. 'Map B. Map showing lines...

Reference table to existing and proposed railways. Scale: 1 inch to about 157 miles. Lithographed by H and C Graham, Ltd.

title: 1 item (originally enclosed with: 'Weekly Summary of the Natural Resources Section No. 23', 23...
description: 1 item (originally enclosed with: 'Weekly Summary of the Natural Resources Section No. 23', 23 March 1946) extracted from FO 371/54136. Japan. Outline map showing prefectures and principal towns; also showing Korea, in outline only. Title cartouche left blank. Scale: 1 inch to 95 miles. 2 insets: Karafuto (now Sakhalin, Russian Federation), scale: 1 inch to 95 miles; Okinawa, scale: 1 inch to 170 miles. Subsequently placed in Foreign Office file number F 6142/95/23, concerning the weekly summaries for March 1946, 12 April 1946.
context: Public Record Office: Maps and plans extracted to flat storage from various series of records held at the Public Record Office, Kew, from 1977.

title: 1 item extracted from FO 371/6629. 'Map of China shewing Railways and Cables': marking railways...
description: 1 item extracted from FO 371/6629. 'Map of China shewing Railways and Cables': marking railways open to traffice, under construction and under discussion by foreign consortiums; also showing the boundaries with and parts of the railway systems in Burma (now also Myanmar, Korea, Mongolia and Tonkin (now part of Vietnam). Reference table; notes on the dates and place names of the first trains. Printed by Ordnance Survey 1921. Insets: 'Sketch Map of Manchuria', scale: 1 inch to 115 miles; 'Sketch Map showing Railway Connections around Peking', scale: 1:200,000.
